items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has begun entering the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: LIVE: Penn Station as Long Island Rail Road strike resumes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live from the picket line at Penn Station after a strike shut down the Long Island Rail Road, the busiest commuter rail system in North America. About a quarter million people use LIRR trains each weekday to commute between New York City and its Long Island suburbs. Read more: https://bit.ly/4fskFKq

#pennstation</article>

items.title: Video captures aftermath of two Navy jets colliding and crashing during Idaho air show
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>All four crew members ejected safely after two Navy jets collided and crashed Sunday during an air show at the Mountain Home Air Force Base in western Idaho, officials said.

The aircraft were performing an aerial demonstration when the crash occurred, a spokesperson for Naval Air Forces, U.S. Pacific Fleet, said in a statement.

#shorts #navyjet #crash #collision #aftermath #idaho</article>

items.title: LIVE: Gaza flotilla view as Israeli troops intercept ships trying to breach blockade
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Live view from boats that are part of a flotilla attempting to break the Gaza blockade after the Israeli military began intercepting them.</article>

items.title: Russia's Putin to visit China days after Trump met with Xi Jinping
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russia's President Vladimir Putin will visit China Tuesday and Wednesday this week, the Chinese Foreign Minister announced.

items.title: Africa CDC chief says he's in 'panic mode' after Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The absence of approved therapeutics or vaccines for the Bundibugyo virus, a rare variant of the Ebola disease, has caused concerns among health authorities in Africa amid the active cases. Dr Jean Kaseya, the Director-General of Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention, emphasized the need of manufacturing vaccines and medicines in Africa.

#ebola #congo #uganda #news

items.title: Three Palestinians killed in Israeli strike on Gaza soup kitchen
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Three Palestinians were killed in an Israeli strike on a soup kitchen in central Gaza on Sunday, according to officials at the hospital where the bodies were brought.

items.title: Iraq's special forces take journalists to site of reported Israeli outpost
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Iraqi military took journalists to the site of a reported Israeli outpost in the desert on Sunday to show there was no indication of a long-term military presence at the site. (AP video shot by: Ali Jabar)

items.title: Congo health minister says Ebola patients receiving care after outbreak
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>DRC health minister Samuel Roger Kamba said on Sunday that authorities had already selected sites in Rwampara and Mongwalu to establish new treatment centers and that dozens of patients were receiving active care.

#ebola #congo #africa #health

items.title: Eurovision winner Dara arrives in Bulgaria after winning with her song 'Bangaranga'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of fans waving Bulgarian flags gathered at Sofia Airport as the singer arrived carrying the Eurovision trophy after winning with her song “Bangaranga.” The victory marked Bulgaria’s first-ever win in the Eurovision Song Contest, sparking celebrations across the country.

#eurovision #eurovision2026 #dara

items.title: Hochul urges unions to resume talks as largest US commuter rail system shuts down
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>New York Gov. Kathy Hochul begged unions representing workers for the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, to resume bargaining Sunday, saying the workers and hundreds of thousands of commuters stand to lose if a strike drags on.

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Italian President, Sergio Mattarella, and Prime Minister, Giorgia Meloni, visited a hospital on Sunday treating those injured after a car collided with pedestrians in the city of Modena.

items.title: Drone strikes UAE nuclear power plant in blow to Iran ceasefire
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A drone strike targeted the United Arab Emirates’ sole nuclear power plant on Sunday, sparking a fire on its perimeter. There were no reports of injuries or radiological release, but it highlighted the risk of renewed war as the Iran ceasefire remains tenuous.

Photo credit: Emirates News Agency

items.title: Indigenous leader says US-Mexico border wall is ruining sacred sites
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Indigenous leaders say U.S. government contractors in the rush to expand the wall are desecrating Native American sacred places and cultural sites at an unprecedented pace. (AP video shot by Julie Watson/Greg Bull/Javier Arciga; produced by Javier Arciga)
